item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article come from Wikimedia Commons and Public Domain Pictures, both of which are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, it’s strange in a good way. +1

What they should do is take a beetle out and place it in pure alcohol to see if that is a better dampener than Hand sanitizer. And also keep the main bottle in a box insulted with hand sanitizer along with the main containment chamber, to ensure as much blockage of its effect in the event of pressurization as possible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very fun and clever article. I really like how things escalate all the way through, and it felt like you were really taking into account all the things modern people take for granted that would be affected by this anomaly (Eskobar's point notwithstanding).

Another thing I like - sometimes there's a trend in SCPs for things to "make sense" through a kind of magical thinking lens, where things are just a little too pat - if someone else had written this they might have put in glass beads instead of beetles. There's a lot of disparate and seemingly unrelated elements here (beetles? In alcohol? That attract sand?) but they're very simple, so it's very weird but easy to read and understand, and I actually really appreciate that the moving parts here kept me on my toes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My mistake, tags should be updated now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The tag is not completed. Guess you may need to add all possible tags to it, or if you are not sure what to do with the tags, you can remove all the tags and the tag team will handle it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading!

The lid of the container is not sealed at all to allow access into the bottle. It could certainly be sealed in the future if deemed necessary but as for right now it's not incredibly important.

Also this is my first SCP so thank you for your kind words!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job with this scp and interesting containment procedure (using hand sanitizer) I don't know if this is your first scp or not but really well done. I just have one tiny question in the containment procedure if you seal the lid of scp 3917 with resin indefinitely and then said that you need to top it off how did that work? other wise interesting scp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the input!

I am considering adding details to how the mechanisms were modified to use no computer parts but don't want to go into unnecessary detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, guy. Someone finally found a use for Germ-X that I can get behind. I usually don't get very into animal-based SCPs, because I'm a dunce when it comes to biology, but this is really top-notch in terms of an effect that's limited enough to be interesting but dangerous enough to also be interesting.

One quibble: most dump trucks and cranes also use on-board computers and circuit boards. As do all cars made after…I dunno, 1985, maybe? Not to be a stickler, but you'd probably have to either use well-maintained older models of equipment or use specially built, computerless (or silicon-circuit-board free, at least) equipment. And keep away from traffic, because you'll presumably take out all cars that come into the effect radius as well.
